coordinates_learning_params info: This code learns to build a flag_pattern using an existing coordinate system. This prerequisite coordinate system can be build using the learning_main (learning_params, env=sliding_puzzle_coordinates or env=sliding_puzzle_multiEnvs_coordinates). A same coordinate_system could be used then to learn different flag_patterns, launching different times this code. If you want to launch this code on an existing coordinate system, put the learning path in launch.sh, learning_analysis_dir. Warning: coordinate learning cannot occur without first constructing a coordinate system, i.e. learning ['evolutionary_settings']['env_name'] should be in ['sliding_puzzle_coordinates', 'sliding_puzzle_multiEnvs_coordinates']
evolutionary_settings info:
density and mobility parameters (sliding_puzzle_density, sliding_puzzle_proba_move, ..., see learning_params.json) are copied from learning parameters 
same_as_learning_gradient: if true, evolutionary settings (nb_runs, nb_evals, env_name, flags_distance_mode, nb_intrasteps, see learning_params.json) are copied from learning parameters; if false, the following parameters are used 
nb_runs: number of independent evolutionary learnings to run 
nb_evals: the max budjet of allowed evaluations for one entire learning process 
sliding_puzzle_nb_intrasteps: activate (value: scalar, nb of intrasteps) or not (value: false) the repeated forward NN computation of each robot controller in the space of two subsequent steps

evolutionary_settings.sliding_puzzle_multiEnvs info: same_as_learning_gradient: if true, parameters (env_dims_list, see learning_params.json) are copied from learning parameters; if false, the following parameters are used
evolutionary_settings.flags_distance_mode info: flags_distance_mode: target and swarm images distance in [MSE, SSIM, CLIP] (not well implemented)

environment info:
same_as_learning_gradient: if true, parameters (time_steps, time_window_start, time_window_end, see learning_params.json) are copied from learning parameters; if false, the following parameters are used 
time_steps: total length of the development task 
time_window_start, time_window_end: interval [time_window_start (step), time_window_end (step)] of steps during which we compute the task performance
grid info:
flag_pattern in ['two-bands', 'three-bands', 'centered-disc', 'not-centered-disc', 'centered-half-discs', 'not-centered-half-discs', 'bn-SU', 'bn-smile', 'bn-smile2', 'rgb-italian-flag', 'rgb-french-cockade', 'rgb-rainbow-full', 'rgb-rainbow-arrow'] 
other grid parameters (grid_nb_rows, grid_nb_cols, init_cell_state_value, see learning_params.json) are copied from learning parameters

learning_random_async_update_states.learning_random_async_update_states info: false: agents states are updated in random grid-positions order and asynchronous time, meaning that the updated state value of a chosen agent is used to compute the state of a not yet updated neighbor (realistic robotic setup). true: in grid-positions order, each agent computes its new state using pre-update neighbors state values, then all new agents states are updated synchronously. In both options, all agents states are updated

learning_random_init_states.learning_random_init_states info: false: the initial state of each agent is a fixed vector of init_cell_state_value. true: the initial state of each agent is a list of random values (normal distribution in [0,1[ ).

learning_with_noise.learning_with_noise info: each newly computed agent' state is perturbed by a noise N(0, learning_with_noise_std, len_state)
